name: What should be included in a bathroom remodeling contract?
headline: What should be included in a bathroom remodeling contract?

mainEntity.name: What should be included in a bathroom remodeling contract?
mainEntity.text: What should be included in a bathroom remodeling contract?

acceptedAnswer.text: Your bathroom remodel contract should clearly explain **scope, price, schedule, and responsibilities**. Scope means exactly what work is included: demo, plumbing, electrical, tile, fixtures, painting, and cleanup. It should list what materials are provided by the contractor and which items, if any, you will supply yourself. The price section should show the total cost and, ideally, an itemized breakdown. Look for details on payment schedule—when deposits and progress payments are due—and make sure they’re tied to completed milestones, not just dates. The contract should also cover permits, inspections, and how change orders will work. Change orders are written changes to the original plan, usually with added cost or time. This process protects both you and the contractor. Ask for approximate start and finish dates, how often crews will be on site, and what hours they typically work. A good contract will mention how your property will be protected and how debris will be handled. Pitfalls include signing a vague contract that leaves out important items like waterproofing type, venting, or specific fixture models. If it’s not in writing, don’t assume it’s included. Take the time to read every line, ask questions, and have changes added to the document—not just discussed verbally—before you sign.
